pattern: The lexicographically greatest substring is always a suffix, so a two-pointer race between competing candidate start positions finds the maximal suffix in linear time.
hints: Any substring is dominated by the suffix that starts at the same place, so the answer is just the largest suffix.
Keep two candidate start positions and compare the characters at growing offsets; whichever falls behind is eliminated.
Advance pointers i and j with an offset k: on a tie grow k, otherwise jump the loser past the compared region and reset k, keeping i as the current best start.

edge_inputs.description: all same characters, answer is whole string
repeated block where later start wins

stress.description: random small-alphabet strings to stress tie-breaking
stress.note: small alphabet creates many equal-prefix ties for the two-pointer to resolve
